audit_metadata.auditor: Claude Code Security Audit — Principal AppSec Engineer Methodology
audit_metadata.scope: Full OWASP Top 10 2025 audit across 67 source files (39 API routes, 13 lib modules, 15 components)
audit_metadata.methodology: OWASP ASVS v4.0 + Language-Specific + Business Logic + Race Condition Analysis

key_risks: Non-atomic read-modify-write patterns on points/XP allow race condition exploitation (game votes, submission approval, admin adjustments)
Two-admin approval for large point adjustments is client-asserted with no cryptographic verification — single admin can bypass
Stateless session tokens with 24hr lifetime cannot be revoked; logout is cosmetic only
No rate limiting on authentication endpoints (nonce generation, signature verification) enables brute-force and DoS
Submission approval has no status guard — double-approval awards duplicate points
immediate_actions_required: Add status precondition (.eq('status','submitted')) to admin review UPDATE to prevent double-approval (FINDING-BL-007)
Check nonce UPDATE return data to close the TOCTOU authentication race window (FINDING-BL-001)
Add rate limiting to /api/auth/nonce and /api/auth/verify endpoints (FINDING-A04-002/003)
Use SQL increments (SET col = col + delta) instead of read-modify-write for all points/XP updates (FINDING-BL-002/003/004)
Add quest bonus XP to users.total_xp on auto-approve (FINDING-BL-006)

findings.title: Two-Admin Approval Bypass — Approving Admin Not Cryptographically Verified
findings.description: The two-admin approval for large points adjustments only checks that the approving_admin wallet address exists in the admin list. No cryptographic signature or separate confirmation request is required. A single admin can bypass the dual-control by providing any other admin's wallet address.
findings.evidence: if (parsed.approving_admin === session.walletAddress) { ... } // Only checks different wallet, not actual approval
findings.impact: Single compromised admin can authorize unlimited points adjustments up to 10,000 points, defeating the dual-authorization control entirely.

remediation.recommendation: Implement two-phase approval: Admin A creates pending_adjustment, Admin B calls separate /approve endpoint with their own session.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. The approving_admin field is a plain string in the request body with no authentication binding.

findings.title: Profile Endpoint Exposes Reward Details to Non-Owner Viewers
findings.description: The rewards array (including amounts, claim status, tx signatures) is returned to all authenticated viewers regardless of ownership. Submissions and points are properly filtered but rewards are not.
findings.evidence: return NextResponse.json({ ...rewards, // always returned regardless of isSelf/isAdmin });
findings.impact: Any authenticated user can view another user's payout amounts, claim history, and transaction signatures.

remediation.recommendation: Apply isSelf || isAdmin gate to rewards: const visibleRewards = isSelf || isAdmin ? rewards : [];

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Verified by reading the response construction — rewards is included unconditionally.

findings.title: All Queries Use Service Role Key — No RLS Defense-in-Depth
findings.description: All API routes use the Supabase service role key which bypasses Row-Level Security policies. Authorization is enforced entirely in application code with no database-level defense-in-depth.
findings.evidence: _serviceClient = createClient(supabaseUrl, serviceKey);
findings.impact: Any authorization bug in application code provides unrestricted database access. No second layer of protection.

remediation.recommendation: Enable RLS on all tables. For user-facing endpoints, consider using anon key with user JWT claims for automatic RLS enforcement.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive — architectural concern. Current application-level auth checks appear correct but this is a missing defense layer.

findings.title: Session Token Role Claim Trusted Without Re-verification on Admin Endpoints
findings.description: Admin role is embedded in the session token at login and never re-verified. If an admin is removed from the allowlist, their token grants admin access for up to 24 hours.
findings.evidence: role: isAdmin ? 'admin' : 'member', // baked in at token creation, checked via session.role on each request
findings.impact: Revoked admins retain full admin access until token expires (24 hours).

remediation.recommendation: Re-verify admin status from admin_wallets table on each admin endpoint request. Cache with 5-minute TTL.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Token-based role with no re-verification and 24hr lifetime.

findings.title: PostgREST Filter Injection via .or() String Interpolation of Role Key
findings.description: The memberState.role_key is interpolated into a .or() filter string. PostgREST uses commas to delimit OR conditions, so a role_key containing a comma could inject additional filter clauses. The role_key originates from user input (validated only as z.string().min(1).max(40)).
findings.evidence: questQuery = questQuery.or(`role_key.is.null,role_key.eq.${memberState.role_key}`);
findings.impact: If a role_key with PostgREST syntax characters passes the season_roles validation, an attacker could manipulate quest query results.

remediation.recommendation: Add strict regex validation: z.string().regex(/^[a-z0-9_]+$/).min(1).max(40) on role_key input. Never interpolate into .or() strings.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive with low exploitation likelihood due to season_roles table validation, but the injection vector is real.

findings.title: CSP Allows unsafe-inline and unsafe-eval for Scripts
findings.description: The Content-Security-Policy script-src includes 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval', significantly weakening XSS protection.
findings.evidence: script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' https://www.youtube.com https://s.ytimg.com
findings.impact: If any XSS vector exists, CSP will not prevent exploitation. The inline theme script in layout.tsx requires unsafe-inline.

remediation.recommendation: Use nonce-based CSP. Remove unsafe-eval if not required by production build.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Verified CSP header configuration in next.config.ts.

findings.title: In-Memory Rate Limiters Ineffective in Serverless Deployments
findings.description: Game vote and reward claim rate limiters use in-memory Maps that reset on serverless cold starts and are not shared across instances.
findings.evidence: const voteRateWindow = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();
findings.impact: Rate limits are trivially bypassable in production serverless deployments. The submissions endpoint correctly uses DB-backed rate limiting.

remediation.recommendation: Replace with Supabase-backed rate limiting matching the pattern in src/app/api/submissions/route.ts.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Verified Map-based rate limiting with no external persistence.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on Authentication Nonce Endpoint
findings.description: The nonce generation endpoint has zero rate limiting. Any client can flood it to fill the auth_nonces table unboundedly.
findings.evidence: No rate limit check present in the handler.
findings.impact: DoS via database row exhaustion. Enables unlimited brute-force authentication probing.

remediation.recommendation: Add IP-based or wallet-based rate limiting (max 10 nonce requests per wallet per 5 minutes). Add TTL-based cleanup for auth_nonces.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. No rate limiting code exists in the handler.

findings.title: No Rate Limiting on Authentication Verify Endpoint
findings.description: The signature verification endpoint has no rate limiting, enabling unlimited authentication attempts.
findings.evidence: No rate limit check present in the handler.
findings.impact: Combined with FINDING-A04-002, enables high-volume brute-force authentication. No account lockout mechanism exists.

remediation.recommendation: Add rate limiting per wallet (max 5 verify attempts per 5 minutes). Track failed attempts and implement exponential backoff.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. No rate limiting or lockout code exists.

findings.title: Stateless Session Tokens Cannot Be Revoked — Logout is Cosmetic
findings.description: Sessions are HMAC-signed stateless tokens with 24hr expiry. No server-side session store exists. Logout only clears the cookie — a copied token remains valid.
findings.evidence: DELETE handler: response.cookies.set(AUTH_COOKIE_NAME, '', { maxAge: 0 }); // Client-side only
findings.impact: Stolen session tokens (via XSS, network interception) are valid for up to 24 hours regardless of logout. Admin tokens equally affected.

remediation.recommendation: Implement server-side session storage. On logout, delete the server-side record. Consider 1-hour tokens with refresh rotation.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Verified no server-side session state exists.

findings.title: Nonce Mark-as-Used TOCTOU Race Condition
findings.description: Nonce is checked via SELECT then marked used via UPDATE in two separate operations. The UPDATE return data is not checked — if zero rows were affected (another request already flipped it), the code does not detect this and issues a duplicate session token.
findings.evidence: const { error: markUsedError } = await supabase.from('auth_nonces').update({used:true}).eq('nonce',nonce).eq('used',false); // return DATA not checked, only error
findings.impact: Two concurrent requests with the same nonce can both receive valid session tokens within a ~1-10ms race window.

remediation.recommendation: Use UPDATE ... RETURNING pattern and check that exactly one row was modified: .select().maybeSingle(); if (!markedRow) return error;
remediation.code_example:
const { data: marked } = await supabase.from('auth_nonces').update({used:true}).eq('nonce',nonce).eq('used',false).select().maybeSingle();
if (!marked) return NextResponse.json({error:'Nonce already used'},{status:401});

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. The UPDATE with .eq('used',false) provides partial protection but the result is not checked.

findings.title: Challenge Cookie Not Signed — No Integrity Protection
findings.description: The auth challenge cookie is base64-encoded JSON with no HMAC signature. An attacker could craft a cookie with a modified issuedAt to extend the challenge freshness window.
findings.evidence: export function encodeChallenge(challenge: AuthChallenge): string { return base64UrlEncode(JSON.stringify(challenge)); }
findings.impact: Mitigated by DB-backed nonce validation, but defense-in-depth is violated. Modified issuedAt could extend the challenge window.

remediation.recommendation: Sign the challenge cookie with HMAC using the same mechanism as session tokens.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive with low exploitability due to DB-backed nonce checks.

findings.title: CSRF Origin Validation Compares Against Attacker-Controllable Host Header
findings.description: CSRF validation checks that Origin matches Host, but both come from the same request. This is a self-referential check rather than validation against a configured allowlist.
findings.evidence: const allowedHosts = new Set([host]); // host from request header
findings.impact: In misconfigured proxies or multi-hostname deployments, an attacker who controls the Host header can bypass CSRF protection.

remediation.recommendation: Compare Origin against a configured allowlist: const ALLOWED_ORIGINS = new Set([process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL].map(u => new URL(u).host));

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Behind standard reverse proxies the risk is low, but the validation is architecturally weak.

findings.title: No Authentication Event Logging
findings.description: No structured logging for authentication events — successful logins, failed signature verifications, expired nonces, or wallet mismatches are not logged. Only the catch-all error handler logs.
findings.evidence: No audit_logs entries created for auth events. Only: console.error('Auth verification error:', error);
findings.impact: Impossible to detect brute-force attacks, credential compromise, or investigate security incidents post-facto.

remediation.recommendation: Add structured security event logging for every auth outcome to the audit_logs table.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. No auth event logging exists anywhere in the codebase.

findings.title: No Authorization Failure Logging on Admin Endpoints
findings.description: Non-admin users attempting to access admin endpoints are silently rejected with no logging. Repeated attempts go undetected.
findings.evidence: if (!session || session.role !== 'admin') { return NextResponse.json({error:'Not found'},{status:404}); } // No logging
findings.impact: Privilege escalation attempts are invisible to monitoring.

remediation.recommendation: Log authorization failures on admin endpoints to audit_logs with wallet address, endpoint path, and timestamp.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Verified no logging occurs on admin auth rejection.

findings.title: No Audit Trail for Reward Claims
findings.description: Reward claims (financial operations) do not write to audit_logs. Admin actions log there, but the highest-value user action does not.
findings.evidence: No audit_logs.insert() call in the claim handler.
findings.impact: When claims are enabled, financial operations lack accountability and forensic trail.

remediation.recommendation: Add audit_logs entries following the admin/points pattern: action='reward_claimed', include reward_id, amount, idempotency_key.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Claims are currently feature-gated but logging should be in place before enablement.

findings.title: TOCTOU Race Condition in Auth Nonce Consumption (Duplicate of A07-002)
findings.description: See FINDING-A07-002. SELECT + UPDATE race window allows duplicate session token issuance.
findings.evidence: See FINDING-A07-002.
findings.impact: Nonce replay within ~1-10ms race window.

remediation.recommendation: See FINDING-A07-002.

findings.false_positive_assessment: Duplicate finding confirmed by independent agent.

findings.title: Game Vote Points — Non-Atomic Read-Modify-Write Race Condition
findings.description: Points are read from game_player_state, calculated in JS, then written as absolute value. Two concurrent votes read the same base value, both compute new totals, and the second write overwrites the first — losing points.
findings.impact: Lost points on concurrent votes for different videos. Exploitable in reverse to inflate points.

remediation.recommendation: Use SQL increment: SET points = points + $delta instead of absolute value write.
remediation.code_example: await supabase.rpc('increment_game_state', { p_wallet: walletAddress, p_points_delta: earned, p_new_streak: newStreak });

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Classic lost-update race condition.

findings.title: Submission Approval — Non-Atomic Multi-Step Points/XP Credit
findings.description: Approval reads user.total_xp, calculates new value in JS, writes absolute value. Two concurrent approvals for the same user lose one set of points. The 5-step flow has no transactional boundary.
findings.evidence:
const newTotalXp = (userData?.total_xp || 0) + points;
await supabase.from('users').update({ total_xp: newTotalXp });
findings.impact: Points lost on concurrent approvals. Ledger and user XP can drift permanently.

remediation.recommendation: Use SET total_xp = total_xp + $points. Wrap in Supabase RPC for atomicity. Add reconciliation job.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Same pattern as BL-002.

findings.title: Quest Auto-Approve Does Not Update users.total_xp
findings.description: When quest submissions are auto-approved, bonus points are written to points_ledger but NOT added to users.total_xp. This creates systematic drift between the ledger and displayed XP.
findings.evidence: await supabase.from('points_ledger').insert({...pointsReward...}); // No users.total_xp update
findings.impact: Users who complete quests get shortchanged on visible XP, level, and tier calculations.

remediation.recommendation: After inserting quest bonus to ledger, also increment users.total_xp. Apply in both quest submit and admin review auto-complete.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Verified no users table update occurs on quest auto-approve.

findings.title: Submission Can Be Approved Multiple Times — No Status Guard
findings.description: The admin review endpoint has no status precondition on SELECT or UPDATE. An already-approved submission can be approved again, awarding duplicate points and creating duplicate ledger entries.
findings.evidence: await supabase.from('submissions').update({status:'approved'}).eq('id', id); // No .eq('status','submitted') guard
findings.impact: Direct points inflation vector. Admin can re-approve submissions to multiply points. Also allows approve-after-reject without proper accounting reversal.

remediation.recommendation: Add .in('status', ['submitted','ai_scored','human_review']) to both SELECT and UPDATE. Check update affected exactly one row.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. No status guard exists on the UPDATE query.

findings.title: AI Scoring Prompt Injection — Anomaly Detection Warns But Does Not Block
findings.description: Anomaly detection on AI scores only logs warnings — it does not reject or flag suspicious submissions for human review. Sophisticated prompt injections producing plausible but inflated scores bypass it.
findings.evidence: if (detectScoringAnomaly(breakdown)) { console.warn(...); } // Warn only, no action
findings.impact: Points inflation via prompt injection bounded to ~2x normal (900 → 1800 points per submission).

remediation.recommendation: When anomaly detection fires, set submission status to 'human_review' instead of auto-scoring.

findings.false_positive_assessment: True positive. Good mitigations exist but anomaly detection is advisory-only.

statistics.remediation_effort_estimate: 40-60 hours for all findings; 8-12 hours for HIGH priority only
